steps.title: Build line j from two points
steps.content: Line $k$ is $y=8x+7$, so its slope is $8$. Since line $j$ is parallel to $k$, line $j$ also has slope $8$. The point $(0,15)$ is on line $j$; moving $1$ unit right raises $y$ by $8$, so a second point is $(1,23)$.

steps.title: Find line j from two points
steps.content: Use those two points directly. From $(0,15)$ to $(1,23)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{23-15}{1-0}=8.$$ Substituting $(0,15)$ into $y=8x+b$ gives $b=15$, so line $j$ is $y=8x+15$.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $y=15x+7$: The slope is $15$, not $8$.
  • Choice B — $y=-15x+7$: The slope is $-15$, not $8$.
  • Choice C — $y=8x+15$: The slope is $8$, and the $y$-intercept is $15$.
  • Choice D — $y=-8x+15$: The slope is $-8$, not $8$.
C is correct.
